summary: This notebook provides a complete baseline pipeline for the CSIRO Biomass competition, demonstrating how to train a multi-target regression model on dual-panel aerial imagery. It combines a Vision Transformer (dinoV3) with a custom Mamba-style fusion neck to process split image halves, alongside a SigLIP-based feature extraction module. The author details data cleaning (artifact removal, date stamp inpainting), a 5-fold stratified group validation scheme, and a training recipe using EMA, gradient accumulation, and TTA. Readers should learn how to handle large dual-panel images, derive missing biomass targets post-inference, and structure a reproducible submission workflow.
competition_flow: Loads training/test CSVs and images, preprocesses them by cropping artifacts and splitting into halves, trains a ViT-based model with Mamba fusion across 5 folds, aggregates TTA predictions, derives missing targets, and generates a formatted submission CSV.
data_reading: cv2.imread for raw images, pd.read_csv for train/test metadata CSVs
Images are split into left/right halves (1000x1000 each) for dual-input processing
CSV targets are pivoted and melted to align with image paths and sample IDs
data_processing: Resize to 512x512, ImageNet normalization, augmentations (horizontal/vertical flips, random rotation, color jitter)
Custom clean_image function: crop bottom 10%, HSV mask & inpaint orange date stamp artifacts
TTA with 4 deterministic transforms (original, hflip, vflip, both flips)
Gradient accumulation (4 steps), EMA (decay=0.9), gradient checkpointing for VRAM management
features_engineering: SigLIP image embeddings extracted via sliding patches and mean-pooled
Semantic features computed via prompt similarity (positive/negative agronomic descriptions)
Derived targets computed post-hoc with non-negativity constraints (max(0, gdm - green), max(0, total - gdm))
loss_functions: Weighted SmoothL1Loss (Huber, beta=5.0) across 5 targets, combined with R2-based weighting scheme
cv_strategy: StratifiedGroupKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=82947501) with Sampling_Date as groups and State as stratification target
ensembling: Averages predictions across 4 TTA views per fold, then averages across 5 trained folds; post-processing applies non-negativity constraints to derived targets.
insights: Splitting wide images into dual inputs improves feature extraction and model focus.
Removing date stamp artifacts via HSV masking and inpainting prevents model confusion.
Mamba blocks efficiently mix spatial tokens with linear complexity compared to standard attention.
EMA stabilizes validation metrics and yields better generalization during training.
Derived targets can be reliably computed post-inference with simple arithmetic constraints.
critical_findings: Orange date stamps at the bottom consistently cause artifacts and are reliably removed via HSV masking.
Bottom 10% of images contains persistent artifacts and is safely cropped without losing signal.
Gradient checkpointing is necessary to fit the ViT-H model on available Kaggle GPU VRAM.
TTA uses deterministic flips/rotations rather than stochastic augmentation for reproducible inference.
